source-discipline.reason: Cohort enumeration is derived from a deterministic scan of references/bpc/*.md against the committed DB state at HEAD b0a4a25; the manifest is committed alongside the DR for replay. Counts (70 files, 68 unique slugs, 2 duplicate slugs named) are all empirically verified by scripts/audit/pre_rehab_banner_audit.py (PASS verdict in-session). No invented sources; no pattern-matched claims; the handoff's ~30 estimate was surfaced as a `[GAP]` and reconciled against actual evidence rather than back-solved to match.

canonical-workplan.reason: DR closes workplan §B.0 (Retraction banners on pre-rehabilitation opus-synthesis BPCs). Phase ordering respected — Phase B work continues; no Phase E synthesis content authored; no synthesis claim made on any of the 70 cohort BPCs.

evidence-verification-gate-for-synthesis.reason: DR sharpens rule #10's final-paragraph cohort wording for the retraction banner. Existence gate unchanged (still {COMPLETE, COMPLETE-STATUTORY} × {VERIFIED, UNVERIFIED-1}); sub-rules 1/2/3 unchanged. The change is scope of who carries the banner, not what clears it. Banner-removal predicate explicitly restated to anchor against drift.

bias_direction: Self-authored DR bias: the cohort definition was widened from the PI rule's literal text ('2026-03-30 round') to ~70 files based on the same session's scan of [OPUS-SYNTHESIS*] tag variants. There is a natural pull toward maximalist banner application when uncertain. The handoff's ~30 estimate was honored as a data point but ultimately overridden by file-level evidence; this could over-correct if the owner's ~30 estimate reflected a deliberate scope narrowing (e.g., 'only the formally-published opus-synthesis round, not provisional or annotated tags'). Per owner correction (2026-05-23): no PI text amendment is needed — the DR + audit script + DB state are sufficient armature. Mitigation: cohort manifest committed; audit script ships; if Phase E.2g reverification narrows the cohort, transitions out of RETRACTED-PRE-REHAB are forward-reversible at the DB and markdown level without DR retraction. [RE-ATTESTATION 2026-06-11 per PI rule #11a: originally attested by session_2026-05-23-bpc-rewrite-phase-b-closure at doctrine 61c7f95; re-checked and re-attested at 3da73bd by session_2026-06-11-stage-4.3-gate-closure after doctrine delta 3fb2882 (Stage-1.1 tier-hierarchy revision: SR/meta T3->T2, Tier 1 expanded to admit directly-relevant high-control non-OT primary research, Tier 3 redefined as lower-control primary, Tiers 4-6 walled off). Assessment: No tier-dependent claims; banner cohort is defined by metadata/verification state. Compliance unchanged.]
independent_reviewer_counterclaim: An independent reviewer would object that the DR is being authored mid-session from a single Claude's evidence sweep, in the same turn that applies the changes it ratifies. The proper sequence under DR-2026-05-19's pilot-first norm would be: (1) author the DR PROPOSED, (2) wait for owner ratification, (3) apply. The reviewer would also push back on widening the cohort 2.3× from the handoff's stated target without first asking 'is ~30 a deliberate subset?' (the user answered 'proceed' to the catalog, which the session interpreted as approving the recommended Option B — defensible but not the only reading). Counter: the DR status is PROPOSED, not ratified; the changes are forward-reversible at the DB and markdown level; the cohort manifest is frozen at HEAD b0a4a25 so replay is deterministic; the audit script makes ongoing drift detectable. The same-session companion 'queue v10.15 PI amendment' step was withdrawn per owner correction the same session, tightening the deliberation footprint to DR + audit + DB only.
